page: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. The server is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and processing capabilities. The server achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and an image processing score of 15819.0, both falling into the strong performance tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 43.73 GB/sec. In contrast, LLM text generation performance scales down with model size, delivering a strong 296.4 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, but dropping to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec for a large 70B model. Other metrics, such as the stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 45418.32 and the Passmark database operations score of 21120.59, represent average tier performance.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and substantial local storage, this server offers qualitative resource density for memory-bound workloads without requiring GPU acceleration. The combination of dedicated physical cores, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage makes it highly suitable for database operations, memory-intensive web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale text processing or inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, featuring 24 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on an x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and a static web serving throughput of 43.73 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation performance is weak for larger 70B models, yielding 2.74 tokens/sec. With a high 1:8 memory-to-core ratio and dedicated CPU resources, this server is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 core-to-RAM ratio
